seoTitle: How to Set Up Owner's Equity in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Set up owner's or partner's equity accounts in QuickBooks Online's Chart of Accounts to track your business's financial health.
intro: Owner's or partner's equity reflects what's actually left in the business after liabilities, which makes it a key figure for financial planning and tax strategy. This guide covers setting it up correctly within QuickBooks Online's Chart of Accounts. It's aimed at small business owners, partners, and accountants who need this piece of their books in order.

steps.heading: Access the Chart of Accounts
steps.body: Navigate to the Chart of Accounts section in QuickBooks Online where account types are managed.

steps.heading: Set up owner's equity
steps.body: Create an equity account specifically for tracking owner or partner contributions and withdrawals.

steps.heading: Avoid common mistakes
steps.body: Make sure equity transactions aren't accidentally categorized as income or expenses.

steps.heading: Review key takeaways
steps.body: Confirm the equity account reflects an accurate picture of the business's financial position.

faq.question: What's the difference between owner's equity and partner's equity?
faq.answer: Owner's equity applies to a sole proprietorship, while partner's equity is used when there are multiple owners sharing the business.

faq.question: Can I have separate equity accounts for each partner?
faq.answer: Yes, setting up individual equity accounts per partner is common for tracking contributions separately.

faq.question: Does equity setup affect tax reporting?
faq.answer: Accurate equity tracking supports better tax planning, though specific tax treatment should be confirmed with an accountant.
